label: sharp-dual
headline_statement: Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:sampling}--\ref{ass:regular}, the available assumptions imply the computable weak Spearman-band dual certificate, but they do not prove the conjectured no-gap equalities.  For \(P_X\)-almost every \(x\) and every \(g\in\{h_x,-h_x\}\), \[D_x(g)\le \inf_{\gamma\in\Gamma_x(\rho_-,\rho_+)}\int g\,d\gamma,\] where \[D_x(g)=\sup_{\lambda_-,\lambda_+\ge0,\ \alpha,\beta\in L^1([0,1])}\left\{\int_0^1\alpha(u)\,du+\int_0^1\beta(v)\,dv+\lambda_-a_- -\lambda_+a_+:\alpha(u)+\beta(v)\le g(u,v)+(\lambda_+-\lambda_-)uv\ \forall(u,v)\in[0,1]^2\right\}.\] Consequently every feasible latent law obeys \[\int D_x(h_x)\,dP_X(x)\le \theta\le \int\{-D_x(-h_x)\}\,dP_X(x).\] This is a partial repair only: the finite two-strata refutation search below finds no counterexample, but the reverse inequalities \(L_x(P)\le D_x(h_x)\) and \(-D_x(-h_x)\le U_x(P)\), the endpoint-attaining measurable selectors, closedness of \(I_x\), and the interpolation step needed for \(\Theta_I(P)=[L_\rho(P),U_\rho(P)]\) remain unproved under the written assumptions.
gap_if_partial: The fragment proves only weak duality: D_x(h_x) is a computable lower certificate and -D_x(-h_x) is a computable upper certificate for every feasible latent law. The conjectured reverse inequalities L_x(P) <= D_x(h_x) and -D_x(-h_x) <= U_x(P) remain unproved because the written assumptions do not include, and the repaired proof does not establish, a moment-constrained bounded-Borel transport strong-duality theorem for the Spearman interval. The original setup-level sharpness clauses also remain open: endpoint-attaining measurable selectors, closedness of I_x, epsilon-selector residuals tending to zero, and interpolation for every value in [L_rho(P),U_rho(P)].
review_repair_notes: Addressed the reviewer-flagged reverse-duality defect by removing the unsupported no-gap claim.
Kept the finite two-strata refutation attempt and recorded that it fails to refute but cannot prove continuous bounded-Borel strong duality.
Retained Kellerer as the method exemplar for the marginal-dual form while avoiding the previously unsupported extension to the Spearman moment interval.
Did not add a proposed assumption because an assumption asserting exact moment-constrained strong duality would be equivalent to the missing conclusion rather than a primitive regularity condition.
The bootstrap shifted-delta critique concerns the sibling inference conjecture and is intentionally not patched in this sharp-dual fragment.
